bullet_points: Compute Wavelet Scattering Transform (WST) coefficients (J=4, L=8) for training maps using kymatio.
Subsample training set if necessary to ensure completion within 1800s; process in small batches to keep RAM < 64 GB.
Train a Random Forest regressor to predict physical parameters (Omega_m, S_8, T_AGN, f_0, Delta z) from WST coefficients.
Compute residuals by fitting a linear model from predicted parameters to WST space and subtracting the predicted component to isolate non-Gaussian structural information.
Save WST residuals and the regressor model to disk.
Implement a 3-layer MLP encoder (hidden: 256, latent: 32-64) and a 2-layer MLP adversarial discriminator.
Train the VIB encoder on WST residuals with beta=1e-3 for the KL term, capped at 50 epochs.
Compute and print correlations between latent z and physical parameters on a held-out set to verify nuisance invariance.
Save the trained VIB encoder weights.
Train a Masked Autoregressive Flow (MAF) on the VIB-compressed latent space, conditioned on standardized physical parameters.
Use true labels for training map conditioning.
Implement "Nuisance-Swap" augmentation: randomly replace conditioning nuisance parameters of 20% of samples with values drawn from the training distribution.
Save the trained CNF model.
Create a primary validation split by holding out 20% of cosmologies to compute partial AUC.
Use extreme-nuisance realizations from the remaining 80% of cosmologies as a diagnostic for nuisance invariance.
Calibrate NLL scores such that the 5th percentile of InD samples maps to a low OoD score.
Save calibration parameters.
Process 10,000 test maps through the pipeline; print elapsed time after each stage (WST, VIB, CNF).
Fallback: if WST extraction exceeds 300s, reduce to J=3.
Compute OoD scores (NLL) and uncertainty estimates; print min, max, mean, and std of scores to check for degeneracy.
Generate submission.zip containing submission.json.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Test Set Inference and Submission Generation

bullet_points: Draft the Results section with subsections: (1) WST Feature Analysis, (2) VIB Nuisance Marginalization, (3) CNF Density Estimation, (4) OoD Detection Performance, and (5) Score Distribution Analysis.
Interpret the performance of the VCSF method, citing all quantitative diagnostics (correlations, partial AUC, score statistics) from previous steps.
Discuss the effectiveness of the VIB-based nuisance marginalization and the overall robustness of the approach.
